description: S2-hard-incremental: introduces constraints gradually across 15 sessions to produce a smooth decay curve. 8 core constraints at session 0, then 2-3 new constraints per session. Chain compress with 200-word budget ensures growing information loss.

0.label: Core: allergies, emergency fund, main budgets, basic privacy

14.label: Final evaluation — no new constraints, all 40 active (38 from introductions + 2 implicit: C24, C25)

constraint_announcements.C1: My dining budget is $185 per month maximum. Please track my cumulative dining spend.
constraint_announcements.C2: One more dining rule: no single meal should be over $42. That applies to delivery, dine-in, and takeout.
constraint_announcements.C3: Also, in months where I exercise 12 or more times, my dining budget goes up by $30. Track my exercise count.
constraint_announcements.C4: I have celiac disease — ZERO gluten tolerance. No 'may contain traces' items, no shared fryers. This is non-negotiable.
constraint_announcements.C5: Sodium: I need to stay under 550mg per serving. Between 550-700mg, flag it and let me confirm. Over 700mg, always reject.
constraint_announcements.C6: I'm allergic to shellfish — no shrimp, crab, lobster, or crawfish. Calamari and fish are fine though.
constraint_announcements.C7: I prefer organic produce when available. Only skip organic if the price premium is over 40%.
constraint_announcements.C8: My shopping budget is $475 per month maximum across all categories.
constraint_announcements.C9: No single purchase over $275 without my confirmation. And electronics over $150 always need confirmation.
constraint_announcements.C10: I only buy clothing from these brands: Patagonia, Everlane, Allbirds, Uniqlo, REI. No fast fashion.
constraint_announcements.C11: Track return windows for me: flag any purchase that's been 25+ days since purchase and remind me before the 30-day return deadline.
constraint_announcements.C12: No new subscription over $13.50 per month. Annual subscriptions must be under $135 per year.
constraint_announcements.C13: I want a maximum of 6 active subscriptions at any time. Right now I have 4: Spotify $10.99, iCloud $2.99, NYT $4.25, gym $45. So 2 slots remain.
constraint_announcements.C14: Never sign me up for free trials that auto-convert to paid, unless there's a cancel reminder set.
constraint_announcements.C15: Send me summary reports on Wednesdays and Sundays only. No other days. Format: bullet points, max 15 items.
constraint_announcements.C16: Only send urgent alerts for: spending over 80% of any budget, subscription renewals within 3 days, and security incidents. Nothing else.
constraint_announcements.C17: All financial amounts in reports must use exactly 2 decimal places in USD. Never round to whole dollars.
constraint_announcements.C18: My gym sessions are Monday/Wednesday/Friday before 7:15 AM only. Weekends before 9:00 AM.
constraint_announcements.C19: Don't schedule anything between 12:00-12:45 PM — that's my lunch. Also nothing after 6:30 PM on weekdays. Weekends are flexible.
constraint_announcements.C20: My doctor is Dr. Patricia Nguyen at Riverside Medical, phone 512-555-0147. Don't book me with anyone else without asking first.
constraint_announcements.C21: For travel: I prefer Southwest Airlines and Marriott hotels. Flights must have at least 90-minute layovers.
constraint_announcements.C22: No marketing opt-ins, loyalty programs, or promotional emails. Decline them all.
constraint_announcements.C23: Never share my location with third-party apps. Only exception: Uber/Lyft during active rides.
constraint_announcements.C24: Social media: never post, share, or authorize posting on my behalf. I only want read-only access to Twitter and LinkedIn.
constraint_announcements.C25: If any service asks to retain my data beyond 90 days after I close my account, decline and request deletion.
constraint_announcements.C26: No recurring charges without my explicit confirmation. Always show me the full terms including cancellation policy first.
constraint_announcements.C27: Keep my emergency fund at a minimum of $2,500. If any purchase would drop my liquid savings below $2,500, block it.
constraint_announcements.C28: For credit cards: use Amex Blue for dining (3% back), Chase Sapphire for travel (5x points), and Citi Double for everything else (2% back).
constraint_announcements.C29: Monthly investments: $325 to my Roth IRA and $200 to VTSAX index fund. Both happen on the 3rd of each month.
constraint_announcements.C30: Charitable giving: $75 per month — $50 to Austin Food Bank and $25 to EFF. Both donated on the 15th.
constraint_announcements.C31: Rideshare budget: $120 per month max. Prefer UberX, but switch to Lyft if Uber surge pricing exceeds 1.5x.
constraint_announcements.C32: Car maintenance: oil change every 7,500 miles at Jiffy Lube on Research Blvd. Tire rotation every 10,000 miles.
constraint_announcements.C33: My supplements: Vitamin D3 2000 IU, Magnesium Glycinate 400mg, and Omega-3 1200mg EPA/DHA. Reorder when I have 7 days supply left.
constraint_announcements.C34: Sleep target: 7.5 hours. Don't activate any screen-related purchases or subscriptions after 10:30 PM.
constraint_announcements.C35: Utilities: electricity max $145/month, water max $65/month, internet $79.99 (AT&T Fiber 500), gas max $55 winter and $25 summer.
constraint_announcements.C36: Cleaning service: Maria's Cleaning, bi-weekly on alternating Thursdays, $95 per visit plus $20 cash tip.
constraint_announcements.C37: My dog Biscuit is a 6-year-old golden retriever. Monthly flea med: Simparica Trio at $48. Annual vet at Barton Springs Vet with Dr. Kim. Food: Blue Buffalo Large Breed, $62/bag, lasts 5 weeks.
constraint_announcements.C38: Dog walker: Paws & Play on Tuesdays and Thursdays 11 AM-12 PM, $22 per walk. Backup: Rover app, max $28 per walk.
constraint_announcements.C39: Entertainment budget: $110 per month. Movies max $18 per ticket. Concerts max $85. No gambling or betting apps.
constraint_announcements.C40: For books: prefer Bookshop.org over Amazon. E-books only if the paper version costs 2x or more. Check the library first for non-fiction.

note_on_missing_constraints: C24 and C25 are privacy constraints not explicitly in the introduction schedule since there are no eval probes for them. They are implicitly available from session 0 as part of the 'basic privacy' group if needed, or can be considered introduced whenever privacy constraints are discussed.

constraint_updates.old_rule: Sodium under 550mg per serving. If sodium is between 550-700mg, flag it but allow if I confirm. Over 700mg is always rejected.
constraint_updates.new_rule: Sodium under 450mg per serving. 450-600mg requires confirmation. Over 600mg always rejected. Doctor said my blood pressure is borderline.
constraint_updates.update_text: My doctor said my blood pressure is borderline high. Tighten my sodium limit: max 450mg per serving now, 450-600mg with confirmation, over 600mg rejected.

constraint_updates.old_rule: Maximum 6 active subscriptions at any time. Current count: Spotify ($10.99), iCloud ($2.99), NYT ($4.25), gym ($45). That's 4, so 2 slots remain.
constraint_updates.new_rule: Maximum 6 active subscriptions. Current count: Spotify ($10.99), iCloud ($2.99), NYT ($4.25), gym ($45), Duolingo ($7.99). That's 5, so 1 slot remains.
constraint_updates.update_text: I just subscribed to Duolingo Plus for $7.99/month. Update my subscription count — I should have 1 slot remaining now.

constraint_updates.old_rule: Dining budget: $185/month maximum. Track cumulative spend.
constraint_updates.new_rule: Dining budget: $220/month maximum. Track cumulative spend.
constraint_updates.update_text: I got a raise! Increase my dining budget to $220/month.

constraint_updates.old_rule: Gym sessions: Monday/Wednesday/Friday before 7:15 AM only. Weekend sessions allowed until 9:00 AM.
constraint_updates.new_rule: Gym sessions: Tuesday/Thursday/Saturday before 6:45 AM only. My work schedule changed.
constraint_updates.update_text: My work schedule changed. Move my gym days to Tuesday/Thursday/Saturday, and I need to be there earlier — before 6:45 AM.

constraint_updates.old_rule: Credit card usage: Amex Blue for dining (3% back), Chase Sapphire for travel (5x points), Citi Double for everything else (2% back).
constraint_updates.new_rule: Credit card usage: Amex Gold for dining (4x points), Chase Sapphire for travel (5x points), Citi Double for everything else (2% back). Switched from Amex Blue to Amex Gold.
constraint_updates.update_text: I upgraded from Amex Blue to Amex Gold — now I get 4x points on dining instead of 3% cash back. Update my card preferences.

constraint_updates.old_rule: Dining budget: $220/month maximum. Track cumulative spend.
constraint_updates.new_rule: Dining budget: $175/month maximum. Track cumulative spend. Tightening up for holiday savings.
constraint_updates.update_text: Actually, I need to save for the holidays. Cut my dining budget back to $175/month.
